expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: analysis.md (94 lines) and architecture.md (52 lines) both contain substantive content

expectations.text: A1: Correct architecture recommendation (simple style with evolution)
expectations.evidence: Selected: Modular Monolith deployed as container with auto-scaling. Includes migration triggers for future service extraction.

expectations.text: A2: Ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Only 3 styles evaluated: Modular Monolith, Serverless Functions, Microservices. Missing all 5 remaining standard styles: Layered, Microkernel, Service-Based, Service-Oriented, Event-Driven, Space-Based. Also 'Serverless Functions' is not one of the 8 standard architecture styles.

expectations.text: A3: Fitness function table with style-specific structural FFs (not just characteristic FFs)
expectations.evidence: Structural FFs present: FF-1 Deployment Simplicity (artifact count must equal 1 — single deployment invariant), FF-2 Module Coupling (0 cross-module internal imports — module boundary enforcement), FF-5 Module Extraction Readiness (modules testable in isolation). At least 3 structural FFs enforcing modular monolith invariants.

expectations.text: A4: Style fitness functions are specific to the selected style (modular monolith)
expectations.evidence: FFs enforce modular-monolith-specific invariants: single deployment artifact (wouldn't apply to microservices), zero cross-module internal imports (module boundary enforcement), per-module isolation testing (monolith-specific concern for future extractability). FF-3 (stateless endpoints) is more generic but supports the container-based modular monolith model.

expectations.text: A5: Fitness functions include Tool/Approach or implementation guidance
expectations.evidence: Each FF has a Measurement field: CI/CD pipeline artifact count, static analysis/architecture linting (ArchUnit, deptry, eslint boundaries plugin), multi-instance integration test behind load balancer, k6/artillery load test, per-module isolated CI job.

expectations.text: A6: Fitness functions scoped to current phase, future FFs mentioned but not mandatory
expectations.evidence: FFs are scoped to current MVP phase (good). Migration triggers are defined (single module >60% compute, team >3 devs, deployment blocking). However, future phase fitness functions are NOT mentioned at all — only migration triggers. The requirement states future phase FFs should be mentioned but not as mandatory now.
